AVAV.headline: AeroVironment, Inc. Notice of July 27, 2026 Application Deadline for Class Action Lawsuit - Contact Lewis Kahn, Esq. at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, Before Application Deadline
AVAV.description: A class action securities lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly failing to disclose material information between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026. The company is accused of understating competition for U.S. Space Force contracts and overstating its business prospects. Investors who suffered losses during this period have until July 27, 2026 to request lead plaintiff status.

AVAV.headline: Why Unusual Machines Stock Jumped Today
AVAV.description: Unusual Machines (UMAC) stock surged 16.02% on Tuesday, driven by a rally in drone stocks following AeroVironment's impressive 133% revenue growth earnings report. The stock was also boosted by its inclusion in the Russell 2000 Index, which increased visibility with institutional investors.

AVAV.headline: AVAV IMPORTANT DEADLINE: ROSEN, HIGHLY REGARDED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages AeroVironment, Inc. Investors with Losses in Excess of $100K to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - AVAV
AVAV.description: Rosen Law Firm announces securities class action lawsuits against AeroVironment, Futu Holdings Limited, and TruBridge, Inc. AeroVironment is accused of making false statements about competition risks related to U.S. Space Force contracts. Investors with losses exceeding $100,000 are encouraged to join the class actions before the July 27, 2026 lead plaintiff deadline.

AVAV.headline: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges AeroVironment, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
AVAV.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. alleging that the company made materially false and misleading statements regarding its business prospects. Specifically, the complaint claims AeroVironment understated competition risks related to the SCAR program and U.S. Space Force modernization efforts, thereby overstating its financial prospects. Investors who purchased AeroVironment securities between June 25, 2025 and March 10, 2026 are eligible to join the case, with a le

AVAV.headline: Is AeroVironment Inc a Buy After Its Latest Earnings Report?
AVAV.description: AeroVironment crushed analyst estimates in fiscal 2026 Q4 with quarterly revenue doubling to $641.6M and net income surging to $63M. The company's backlog ballooned to $1.2B, and management guided for fiscal 2027 revenue between $2.13B-$2.23B. Growth was driven by both organic expansion and recent acquisitions including BlueHalo and Empirical Systems Aerospace.

AVAV.headline: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Aerovironment, Inc. Investors They Have Until July 27th to Contact the Firm Seeking Lead Plaintiff Role
AVAV.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against AeroVironment, Inc. for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding competition for its Satellite Communication Augmentation Resource (SCAR) program work. The company faced a U.S. government stop work order in January 2026, causing a 15% stock decline, followed by a contract termination announcement in March 2026 that triggered a 6.24% drop and a $151.3 million goodwill impairment.

IRDM.headline: Firefly Aerospace vs. Rocket Lab USA: Which Space Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
IRDM.description: Firefly Aerospace and Rocket Lab USA represent two different approaches to the expanding space economy. Firefly achieved 135% revenue growth in FY 2025 with successful lunar missions, while Rocket Lab is pursuing an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications to become a comprehensive space systems provider. The article recommends Rocket Lab for 2026 despite both companies showing promise, citing its potential to compete with SpaceX through the transformative Iridium deal.

IRDM.headline: Stock Market Today, June 30: Rocket Lab Soars on Acquisition and Major Indexes Gain
IRDM.description: Major stock indexes closed higher on June 30, 2026, with the S&P 500 rising 0.79% and the Nasdaq Composite gaining 1.52%, marking the strongest quarter since 2020. Tech and industrial sectors led gains, while Rocket Lab surged following its $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications. Intel also rose 6% amid AI stock momentum. Gold prices slipped slightly, and analysts suggest further rallies are possible in July.

BB.headline: Stock Market Outlook: Tesla Buy Signal, BlackBerry Surges
BB.description: As markets approach the end of Q2 and H1, trading is expected to be quieter due to holiday-shortened weeks in Canada and the US. The NASDAQ is positioned as the primary market leader. Technical analysis highlights Tesla's new buy signal and BlackBerry's continued strength, while several telecom and mining stocks show weakness. Bitcoin conditions are improving.

CBRS.headline: Is Cerebras Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Surges?
CBRS.description: Cerebras shares declined after reporting strong Q1 revenue growth of 92% year-over-year to $193.4 million and securing major partnerships with OpenAI ($20 billion deal) and AWS. However, investors were disappointed by gross margin guidance forecasting a decline to 38-41% for the year. Despite impressive growth projections and strategic partnerships, the stock remains highly speculative given its $40 billion market cap relative to projected sub-$900 million annual revenue.

CBRS.headline: SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Hub Group, Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations; Investors Should Contact Block & Leviton To Learn How They Might Recover Their Losses
CBRS.description: Hub Group, Inc. faces a securities fraud lawsuit after restating financial statements due to approximately $77 million in understatements of purchased transportation costs and accounts payable. The company's stock fell 18% in February 2026 and an additional 13% in May 2026 following disclosure of material misstatements in its 2023, 2024, and 2025 financial reports. Investors who purchased shares between April 28, 2023, and May 11, 2026, may be eligible to recover losses.

ABVX.headline: Abivax announces trading resumption of its ordinary shares on Euronext Paris
ABVX.description: Abivax SA resumed trading of its ordinary shares on Euronext Paris on July 1, 2026, following a temporary halt. The company announced the pricing of an oversubscribed and upsized public offering of American Depositary Shares, increased from the previously announced $600M to $800M at $125.00 per share.

ABVX.headline: Abivax annonce la suspension temporaire du cours de ses actions ordinaires sur Euronext Paris
ABVX.description: Abivax SA announced a temporary suspension of its ordinary shares trading on Euronext Paris starting July 1, 2026 at market opening. The suspension is related to the company's public offering of American Depositary Shares (ADS) in the United States to confirm investor allocations and announce final offering terms. Trading is expected to resume around 15:30 CEST the same day.

ABVX.headline: Abivax announces temporary trading halt of its ordinary shares on Euronext Paris
ABVX.description: Abivax SA announced a temporary trading halt of its ordinary shares on Euronext Paris effective July 1, 2026, from market opening at 9:00 a.m. CEST. The halt is in connection with the company's public offering of American Depositary Shares in the United States to allow for allocation confirmation and pricing announcement. Trading is expected to resume at approximately 3:30 p.m. CEST the same day.

RKLB.headline: Firefly Aerospace vs. Rocket Lab USA: Which Space Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
RKLB.description: Firefly Aerospace and Rocket Lab USA represent two different approaches to the expanding space economy. Firefly achieved 135% revenue growth in FY 2025 with successful lunar missions, while Rocket Lab is pursuing an $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications to become a comprehensive space systems provider. The article recommends Rocket Lab for 2026 despite both companies showing promise, citing its potential to compete with SpaceX through the transformative Iridium deal.

RKLB.headline: Stock Market Today, June 30: Rocket Lab Soars on Acquisition and Major Indexes Gain
RKLB.description: Major stock indexes closed higher on June 30, 2026, with the S&P 500 rising 0.79% and the Nasdaq Composite gaining 1.52%, marking the strongest quarter since 2020. Tech and industrial sectors led gains, while Rocket Lab surged following its $8 billion acquisition of Iridium Communications. Intel also rose 6% amid AI stock momentum. Gold prices slipped slightly, and analysts suggest further rallies are possible in July.

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Just Made a Move That Could Change Everything
RKLB.description: Rocket Lab announced a major acquisition of satellite telecom giant Iridium for $8 billion, a strategic move that could significantly reshape the company's growth trajectory. The acquisition drew comparisons to SpaceX's strategic playbook and was well-received by the market, with RKLB stock rising 2.92% on the news.

ASTS.headline: AST SpaceMobile vs. Boeing: Which Technology Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
ASTS.description: AST SpaceMobile, a satellite broadband pioneer with major telecom partnerships, is compared against Boeing, which has returned to profitability after years of losses. AST offers high growth potential but faces significant cash burn and regulatory risks, while Boeing provides diversified operations and stability but carries high debt and production challenges. The article recommends Boeing for conservative investors seeking turnaround potential, while aggressive investors may prefer AST's growth

ALAB.headline: Even CEOs Need Cash: Insider Selling Is Not the Only Signal in AI Stocks
ALAB.description: The article argues that insider selling in AI stocks should not be viewed as a bearish signal, as executives often sell through pre-arranged trading plans for tax and diversification purposes. Instead, institutional buying and analyst sentiment are more reliable indicators. While NVIDIA, Snowflake, Astera Labs, Datadog, and CoreWeave show strong institutional support and bullish analyst trends, some stocks like Datadog and Astera Labs may be vulnerable to pullbacks due to recent sharp rallies. T

GLW.headline: The AI Boom Has a Second Act—And It’s Playing Out in Optics
GLW.description: While AI infrastructure investment focuses on chips, the real opportunity lies in optical networking components. Companies manufacturing fiber, connectors, and routing systems are landing multi-billion-dollar contracts with hyperscalers and facing supply bottlenecks. Three key players—Amphenol, Corning, and Ciena—are positioned to benefit from the $1 trillion AI infrastructure buildout, with analyst estimates expected to be revised higher as demand accelerates through 2030.
